name: D. V. Gundappa
bio: Devanahalli Venkataramanayya Gundappa, popularly known as DVG, was a prominent Kannada philosopher and writer. His most famous work is Mankuthimmana Kagga.

name: Kuvempu
bio: Kuppali Venkatappa Puttappa, widely known as Kuvempu, is the first Kannada writer to receive the Jnanpith Award. He is a 'Rastra Kavi'.

name: D.R. Bendre
bio: Dattatreya Ramachandra Bendre is considered one of the greatest Kannada lyric poets of the 20th century. He was awarded the Jnanpith for 'Nakutanti'.

name: Shivaram Karanth
bio: A giant of Kannada literature, novelist, playwright, and environmentalist. Known as 'Walking Encyclopedia' of Karnataka.

name: Masti Venkatesha Iyengar
bio: Popularly known as 'Maasthi Kannadada Aasti' (Masti is Kannada's treasure). He was a prolific short story writer.

name: V. K. Gokak
bio: Vinayaka Krishna Gokak was a writer in the Kannada language and a scholar of English and Kannada literatures.

name: U. R. Ananthamurthy
bio: A contemporary writer and critic in the Kannada language. He is considered one of the pioneers of the Navya movement.

name: Girish Karnad
bio: An Indian actor, film director, Kannada writer, playwright and a Rhodes Scholar, who predominantly worked in South Indian cinema and Bollywood.

name: Chandrashekhara Kambara
bio: A prominent poet, playwright, folklorist, and film director in Kannada language. He is the founder-vice-chancellor of Kannada University, Hampi.

name: Basavanna
bio: A 12th-century philosopher, statesman, and social reformer. He was the founding saint of the Lingayat movement and a pioneer of Vachana Sahitya.

name: Akka Mahadevi
bio: One of the early female poets of the Kannada language and a prominent figure in the Veerashaiva Bhakti movement of the 12th century.

name: Sarvajna
bio: A 16th-century Kannada poet, philosopher and social reformer. He is famous for his three-line Tripadi poems that touch upon every aspect of life.

name: Kanaka Dasa
bio: A poet, philosopher, musician and social reformer from 16th century Karnataka. He was a Haridasa who composed Keerthanas in simple Kannada.

name: Purandara Dasa
bio: Known as the 'Pitamaha' of Carnatic music. He was a Haridasa who made significant contributions to music and Kannada literature.

name: Allama Prabhu
bio: A 12th-century mystic and Vachana poet. He was the president of Anubhava Mantapa, the academy of mystics founded by Basavanna.

name: B.M. Srikantaiah
bio: Known as 'Kannadada Kanva', he spearheaded the 'Navodaya' movement in Kannada literature through his translations and original works.

name: G.S. Shivarudrappa
bio: A poet, critic and academic who was the third writer to be honored as 'Rastra Kavi' by the Government of Karnataka.

name: K.S. Narasimhaswamy
bio: Famous as the poet of love and household life, his collection 'Mysore Mallige' is one of the most successful books in Kannada history.

name: P. Lankesh
bio: An editor, filmmaker, and writer. He was a major voice in the 'Modernist' (Navya) and 'Dalit/Protest' movements in Kannada.

name: Gopalakrishna Adiga
bio: Considered the pioneer of the 'Navya' (Modernist) movement in Kannada poetry, his works introduced new metaphors and styles.

name: Siddalingaiah
bio: One of Karnataka's foremost Dalit poets. His poetry was a catalyst for the Dalit-Bandaya movement, focusing on social justice.

name: M. Govinda Pai
bio: The first poet to be honored with the 'Rastra Kavi' title. He was a polyglot, historian, and a prominent poet of the Navodaya period.

name: Chennavira Kanavi
bio: Often described as the 'Poet of Friendships' (Suneeta), his poetry is known for its mildness, depth, and humanistic touch.

name: Adikavi Pampa
bio: The earliest known Kannada poet, whose works 'Vikramarjuna Vijaya' and 'Adipurana' established the groundwork for Kannada literature in the 10th century.
